title: Use gmail or outlook integration with different domain mail address
excerpt: I want to use or own domain mail to send mails to CRM contacts etc. Monday however only facilitates gmail and outlook integration. I do have a gmail account an di do have a outlook/office365 account however not at the do…

posts.author: PJSamcMD2021 (Paul Swaak)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to use or own domain mail to send mails to CRM contacts etc. Monday however only facilitates gmail and outlook integration. I do have a gmail account an di do have a outlook/office365 account however not at the domain address/mail i want to use in CRM messages? Can this be solved easily?

Looking forward for any suggestions

Paul Swaak

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @PJSamcMD2021 - You can use your own domain with Gmail. That way you get the benefit of all the Gmail integrations in monday․com and other platforms, but your email address still shows @yourcompany.com in the address. Here is Google’s support page about it:

support.google.com

Activate Gmail with Google Workspace (@your-company) - Google Workspace Admin...

After you sign up for Google Workspace and are ready to start using Gmail with your business address, make sure you complete these Gmail setup steps. Required checklist of steps These required

I can assure you it works great. Our company email is handled and hosted through Gmail (and integrated with monday․com) but it has our company domain, like so: deb@polishedgeek.com

~ Deb Cinkus, PMP
Certified monday․com Partner
CEO & Principal Consultant, Polished Geek
